item.title: JSON parse missing try-catch in evolve/ manifest loaders
item.intent: No corrupted manifest file in evolve/ causes an unhandled exception. All manifest loaders recover gracefully with logged warnings and safe defaults.
item.description: Six evolve module manifest loaders use bare JSON.parse without try-catch: src/evolve/role/manifest.ts:54 (loadRoleManifest), src/evolve/provider/manifest.ts:26 (loadProviderManifest), src/evolve/integrate/manifest.ts:26 (loadIntegrationManifest), src/evolve/memory/lifecycle.ts:61, src/evolve/rollback/capability-snapshot.ts:67, src/evolve/skills/rollback.ts:39. These files exist and can be corrupted (disk error, partial write, manual edit). The same pattern was correctly handled in evolve/tools/manifest.ts and evolve/skills/lifecycle.ts (both have try-catch + console.warn). Steps: (1) apply the same try-catch pattern to the 6 missing loaders; (2) fallback: role manifest seeds built-in roles only, others return empty version-1 state; (3) add console.warn(err, context) in each catch; (4) run evolve/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 6 try-catch wrappers across 6 files. Pattern is identical to BUG-012/BUG-015 sweep.
